paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: During the sixth and seventh centuries, the inhabitants of the modern-day states of Gujarat and Rajasthan in north-western India developed a method of gaining access to clean, fresh groundwater during the dry season for drinking, bathing, watering animals and irrigation. However, the significance of this invention – the stepwell – goes beyond its utilitarian application.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Unique to this region, stepwells are often architecturally complex and vary widely in size and shape. During their heyday, they were places of gathering, of leisure and relaxation and of worship for villagers of all but the lowest classes. Most stepwells are found dotted round the desert areas of Gujarat (where they are called vav) and Rajasthan (where they are called baori), while a few also survive in Delhi. Some were located near villages as public spaces for the community; others were positioned beside ponds as resting places for travellers.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: As their name suggests, stepwells comprise a series of stone steps descending from ground level to the water source (normally an underground aquifer) as it recedes following the rains. When the water level was high, the user needed only to descend a few steps to reach it; when it was low, several levels would have to be negotiated.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Some wells are vast, open craters with hundreds of steps paving each sloping side, often in tiers. Others are more elaborate, with long stepped passages leading to the water via several storeys. Built from stone and supported by pillars, they also included pavilions that sheltered visitors from the relentless heat. But perhaps the most impressive features are the intricate decorative sculptures that embellish many stepwells, showing activities from fighting and dancing to everyday acts such as women combing their hair or churning butter.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Down the centuries, thousands of wells were constructed throughout north-western India, but the majority have now fallen into disuse; many are derelict and dry, as groundwater has been diverted for industrial use and the wells no longer reach the water table. Their condition hasn't been helped by recent dry spells: southern Rajasthan suffered an eight-year drought between 1996 and 2004.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: However, some important sites in Gujarat have recently undergone major restoration, and the state government announced in June last year that it plans to restore the stepwells throughout the state.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: In Patan, the state's ancient capital, the stepwell of Rani Ki Vav (Queen's Stepwell) is perhaps the finest current example. It was built by Queen Udayamati during the late 11th century, but became silted up following a flood during the 13th century. But the Archaeological Survey of India began restoring it in the 1960s, and today it is in pristine condition. At 65 metres long, 20 metres wide and 27 metres deep, Rani Ki Vav features 500 sculptures carved into niches throughout the monument. Incredibly, in January 2001, this ancient structure survived an earthquake that measured 7.6 on the Richter scale.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Another example is the Surya Kund in Modhera, northern Gujarat, next to the Sun Temple, built by King Bhima I in 1026 to honour the sun god Surya. It actually resembles a tank (kund means reservoir or pond) rather than a well, but displays the hallmarks of stepwell architecture, including four sides of steps that descend to the bottom in a stunning geometrical formation. The terraces house 108 small, intricately carved shrines between the sets of steps.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Rajasthan also has a wealth of wells. The ancient city of Bundi, 200 kilometres south of Jaipur, is home to one of the larger examples: Raniji Ki Baori, which was built by the queen of the region, Nathavatji, in 1699. At 46 metres deep, 20 metres wide and 40 metres long, the intricately carved monument is one of 21 baoris commissioned in the Bundi area by Nathavatji.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: In the old ruined town of Abhaneri, about 95 kilometres east of Jaipur, is Chand Baori, one of India's oldest and deepest wells; aesthetically it's perhaps one of the most dramatic. Built in around 850 AD next to the temple of Harshat Mata, the baori comprises hundreds of zigzagging steps that run along three of its sides, steeply descending 11 storeys, resulting in a striking pattern when seen from afar. On the fourth side, verandas which are supported by ornate pillars overlook the steps.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Still in public use is Neemrana Ki Baori, located just off the Jaipur-Delhi highway. Constructed in around 1700, it is nine storeys deep, with the last two being underwater. At ground level, there are 86 colonnaded openings from where the visitor descends 170 steps to the deepest water source.

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: Today, following years of neglect, many of these monuments to medieval engineering have been saved by the Archaeological Survey of India, which has recognized the importance of preserving them as part of the country's rich history. Tourists flock to wells in far-flung corners of north-western India to gaze in wonder at these architectural marvels from hundreds of years ago, which serve as a reminder of both the ingenuity and artistry of ancient civilisations and the value of water to human existence.

groups.desc:
Questions 1-5

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: Examples of ancient stepwells can be found all over the world.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“古代的阶梯井在世界各地都有发现”，而原文明确指出“这种结构是该地区独有的”，即阶梯井是现代印度的古吉拉特邦和拉贾斯坦邦这一地区特有的。这与题干所述的信息相反，因此正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Unique to this region, stepwells are often architecturally complex and vary widely in size and shape.
central_sentences.translation: 这种结构独特于这个地区，阶梯井在建筑上通常很复杂，在大小和形状上也有很大的变化。

target_sen.target_sen: Unique to this region, stepwells are often architecturally complex and vary widely in size and shape.
Most stepwells are found dotted round the desert areas of Gujarat (where they are called vav) and Rajasthan (where they are called baori), while a few also survive in Delhi.
Unique to this region, stepwells are often architecturally complex and vary widely in size and shape.

questions.title: Stepwells had a range of functions, in addition to those related to water collection.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“functions”在原文中对应的是“significance”和“places of gathering, of leisure and relaxation and of worship”，而“water collection”对应的是“utilitarian application”。原文明确指出阶梯井的意义超出了其与水相关的实用功能，还包括聚会、休闲、放松和宗教活动。因此，题干中的信息在文章中得到了验证，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: However, the significance of this invention – the stepwell – goes beyond its utilitarian application.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，这项发明——阶梯井——的重要性超越了其实用性。

central_sentences.sentence: During their heyday, they were places of gathering, of leisure and relaxation and of worship for villagers of all but the lowest classes.
central_sentences.translation: 在其鼎盛时期，它们是除最低阶层外所有村民聚会、休闲、放松和崇拜的场所。

target_sen.target_sen: However, the significance of this invention – the stepwell – goes beyond its utilitarian application.
During their heyday, they were places of gathering, of leisure and relaxation and of worship for villagers of all but the lowest classes.
During their heyday, they were places of gathering, of leisure and relaxation and of worship for villagers of all but the lowest classes.
During their heyday, they were places of gathering, of leisure and relaxation and of worship for villagers of all but the lowest classes.
During their heyday, they were places of gathering, of leisure and relaxation and of worship for villagers of all but the lowest classes.
However, the significance of this invention – the stepwell – goes beyond its utilitarian application.

questions.title: The few existing stepwells in Delhi are more attractive than those found elsewhere.
questions.analysis: 题干问德里的现存阶梯井是否比其他地方的更有吸引力。文章只提到德里有少量幸存的阶梯井，但没有提到这些井的吸引力是否比其他地方的更大。因此，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。这个题目考查学生的细致阅读和比较信息的能力。

central_sentences.sentence: Most stepwells are found dotted round the desert areas of Gujarat (where they are called vav) and Rajasthan (where they are called baori), while a few also survive in Delhi.
central_sentences.translation: 大多数阶梯井散布在古吉拉特邦（那里称之为vav）和拉贾斯坦邦（那里称之为baori）的沙漠地区，而在德里也有少数幸存下来的。

syn_ary.synonym: a few also survive in Delhi

target_sen.target_sen: Most stepwells are found dotted round the desert areas of Gujarat (where they are called vav) and Rajasthan (where they are called baori), while a few also survive in Delhi.

questions.title: It took workers many years to build the stone steps characteristic of stepwells.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提到建造台阶井所需的时间。题干中的关键词 "many years" 在文章中没有找到对应的描述。虽然文章详细描述了台阶井的结构和特点，但没有提到建造这些石阶所需的时间。因此，无法确定题干中的信息是否正确。

central_sentences.sentence: one
central_sentences.translation: None

questions.title: The number of steps above the water level in a stepwell altered during the course of a year.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到水位变化会影响需要走的台阶数量。题干中的 "number of steps" 对应文章中的 "descend a few steps" 和 "several levels"，而 "altered" 对应 "when the water level was high" 和 "when it was low"。通过这些对应关系，可以确认题干中的信息在文章中得到了直接的支持。

central_sentences.sentence: When the water level was high, the user needed only to descend a few steps to reach it; when it was low, several levels would have to be negotiated.
central_sentences.translation: 当水位高时，使用者只需下降几级台阶就能到达水面；当水位低时，则需要走过几层台阶。

target_sen.target_sen: When the water level was high, the user needed only to descend a few steps to reach it; when it was low, several levels would have to be negotiated.
When the water level was high, the user needed only to descend a few steps to reach it; when it was low, several levels would have to be negotiated.

syn_ary.synonym: When the water level was high

target_sen.target_sen: When the water level was high, the user needed only to descend a few steps to reach it; when it was low, several levels would have to be negotiated.
When the water level was high, the user needed only to descend a few steps to reach it; when it was low, several levels would have to be negotiated.

groups.desc:
Questions 6-8
Answer the questions below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the text in each gap.

questions.title: Which part of some stepwells provided shade for people?
questions.analysis: 题干问的是“Which part of some stepwells provided shade for people?”，即“哪些部分为人们提供了阴凉”。在原文中，句子“they also included pavilions that sheltered visitors from the relentless heat”明确指出“pavilions”提供了遮蔽（sheltered）游客（visitors）免受酷热（relentless heat）的影响。因此，答案是“pavilions”。

central_sentences.sentence: Built from stone and supported by pillars, they also included pavilions that sheltered visitors from the relentless heat.
central_sentences.translation: 这些建筑由石头建造并由柱子支撑，还包括能够为游客遮挡烈日的凉亭。

target_sen.target_sen: Built from stone and supported by pillars, they also included pavilions that sheltered visitors from the relentless heat.

questions.title: What type of serious climatic event, which took place in southern Rajasthan, is mentioned in the article?
questions.analysis: 答案是“drought”，因为题目问的是“什么类型的严重气候事件发生在南拉贾斯坦邦？”，而文章中明确提到“southern Rajasthan suffered an eight-year drought between 1996 and 2004”，即南拉贾斯坦邦经历了八年的干旱。这里的“drought”就是一种严重的气候事件，因此答案是“drought”。

central_sentences.sentence: Their condition hasn't been helped by recent dry spells: southern Rajasthan suffered an eight-year drought between 1996 and 2004.
central_sentences.translation: 最近的干旱期并没有改善它们的状况：南拉贾斯坦邦在1996年至2004年间经历了长达八年的干旱。

target_sen.target_sen: Their condition hasn't been helped by recent dry spells: southern Rajasthan suffered an eight-year drought between 1996 and 2004.
Their condition hasn't been helped by recent dry spells: southern Rajasthan suffered an eight-year drought between 1996 and 2004.

questions.title: Who are frequent visitors to stepwells nowadays?
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "frequent visitors" 对应原文中的 "flock to"，"nowadays" 对应原文中的 "today"。通过这些同义替换词，可以定位到答案句。答案句明确指出 "Tourists" 是现代频繁访问 stepwells 的人，因此答案是 "tourists"。

central_sentences.sentence: Today, following years of neglect, many of these monuments to medieval engineering have been saved by the Archaeological Survey of India, which has recognized the importance of preserving them as part of the country's rich history. Tourists flock to wells in far-flung corners of north-western India to gaze in wonder at these architectural marvels from hundreds of years ago, which serve as a reminder of both the ingenuity and artistry of ancient civilisations and the value of water to human existence.
central_sentences.translation: 如今，经过多年的疏于管理，这些中世纪工程的纪念碑中的许多已经被印度考古调查局拯救，该机构认识到将它们作为国家丰富历史的一部分加以保护的重要性。游客们涌向印度西北部偏远角落的水井，惊叹于这些数百年前的建筑奇迹，它们不仅展示了古代文明的创造力和艺术性，也提醒人们水对人类存在的重要价值。

target_sen.target_sen: Tourists flock to wells in far-flung corners of north-western India to gaze in wonder at these architectural marvels from hundreds of years ago, which serve as a reminder of both the ingenuity and artistry of ancient civilisations and the value of water to human existence.

Today, following years of neglect, many of these monuments to medieval engineering have been saved by the Archaeological Survey of India, which has recognized the importance of preserving them as part of the country's rich history.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "earthquake"。题干中的 "despite the 【 】of 2001" 表示尽管2001年发生了某个事件，仍然保持良好状态。原文中提到 "Incredibly, in January 2001, this ancient structure survived an earthquake that measured 7.6 on the Richter scale."，这里的 "earthquake"（地震）是2001年发生的事件，并且与题干中的描述完全对应。因此，正确答案是 "earthquake"。

central_sentences.sentence: Incredibly, in January 2001, this ancient structure survived an earthquake that measured 7.6 on the Richter scale.
central_sentences.translation: 令人难以置信的是，在2001年1月，这座古老的建筑物经受住了里氏7.6级地震的考验。

central_sentences.sentence: It actually resembles a tank (kund means reservoir or pond) rather than a well, but displays the hallmarks of stepwell architecture, including four sides of steps that descend to the bottom in a stunning geometrical formation.
central_sentences.translation: 它实际上更像一个水池（kund意为水库或池塘）而不是一口井，但它展现了阶梯井建筑的特征，包括四面台阶下降到底部，形成令人惊叹的几何图形。

The terraces house 108 small, intricately carved shrines between the sets of steps.

It actually resembles a tank (kund means reservoir or pond) rather than a well, but displays the hallmarks of stepwell architecture, including four sides of steps that descend to the bottom in a stunning geometrical formation.
Another example is the Surya Kund in Modhera, northern Gujarat, next to the Sun Temple, built by King Bhima I in 1026 to honour the sun god Surya.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 "tank"。题干中的 "Looks more like a ... than a well" 对应原文中的 "resembles a tank rather than a well"。通过找到这两个同义表达，我们可以确定答案句中的 "tank" 是正确答案。原文明确指出 Surya Kund 更像一个水池（tank），而不是一口井（well），因此答案是 "tank"。

central_sentences.sentence: It actually resembles a tank (kund means reservoir or pond) rather than a well, but displays the hallmarks of stepwell architecture, including four sides of steps that descend to the bottom in a stunning geometrical formation.

central_sentences.sentence: On the fourth side, verandas which are supported by ornate pillars overlook the steps.
central_sentences.translation: 在第四面，由装饰柱支撑的阳台俯瞰着台阶。

target_sen.target_sen: On the fourth side, verandas which are supported by ornate pillars overlook the steps.

Built in around 850 AD next to the temple of Harshat Mata, the baori comprises hundreds of zigzagging steps that run along three of its sides, steeply descending 11 storeys, resulting in a striking pattern when seen from afar.

questions.analysis: 题目中的线索词是“two levels”和“Neemrana Ki Baori”。在原文中，描述Neemrana Ki Baori的句子中提到“the last two being underwater”，这与题目中的“two levels”直接对应。根据这句话，最后两层在水下，因此答案是“underwater”。

central_sentences.sentence: Still in public use is Neemrana Ki Baori, located just off the Jaipur-Delhi highway. Constructed in around 1700, it is nine storeys deep, with the last two being underwater.
central_sentences.translation: 尼姆拉纳阶井仍在公共使用中，位于斋浦尔-德里高速公路附近。它建于1700年左右，深达九层，最后两层在水下。

target_sen.target_sen: Still in public use is Neemrana Ki Baori, located just off the Jaipur-Delhi highway.

content.title: EUROPEAN TRANSPORT SYSTEMS 1990-2010
content.summary: {'A': '高效的交通系统是经济快速增长的重要条件，尽管现代信息技术可以通过促进远程办公和远程服务来减少对实体交通的需求，但交通需求仍在增加，其中乘客交通的决定性因素是汽车使用的惊人增长。', 'B': '欧洲经济和生产体系的变化导致了货物运输的增长，特别是劳动密集型产业的迁移，使得欧盟从“库存”经济转变为“流动”经济。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了预计加入欧盟的候选国家将会出现强劲的经济增长，进而增加运输流量，尤其是公路货运交通。虽然许多候选国家继承了一个鼓励铁路的运输系统，但自1990年代以来，各种运输方式之间的分配已经急剧倾向于公路运输。在1990年至1998年期间，公路货运增长了19.4％，而同期铁路货运减少了43.5％，尽管这可能对扩大的欧盟有利，但仍然平均水平远高于现有成员国。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了可持续发展对欧盟共同交通政策的影响和重要性，强调将环境考虑纳入社区政策，并在交通方式之间寻求平衡是实现可持续发展目标的关键。提出的措施是实现可持续交通系统的首要步骤，最终目标是在2040年前实现。', 'E': '如果不采取措施扭转交通增长趋势，预计到2020年，交通运输所产生的二氧化碳排放量将增加约50%，达到1,1130亿吨，而1990年的排放量为7390亿吨，其中道路交通是主要的罪魁祸首，占交通运输二氧化碳排放量的84%，因此使用替代燃料和提高能源效率既是生态必要性也是技术挑战。', 'F': '当前亟需加快运输模式转型，但受制于过去五十年公路运输的主导地位，转向可持续模式的进程尤为艰难。铁路货运已陷入危机：市场份额萎缩至8%，国际货运列车平均时速仅18公里，濒临边缘化。在此背景下，现已提出三个应对方案。此现状凸显重构货运体系的紧迫性与复杂性，需系统性改革方能突破发展困局。', 'G': '这段话主要讲了如果只通过定价来解决道路交通问题，虽然短期内可能会减缓道路交通的增长，但由于缺乏措施来促进其他交通方式的发展，可持续交通方式无法得到推广。', 'H': '第二种方法主要集中在道路运输定价上，但同时采取措施提高其他交通方式的效率（提供更好的服务质量、物流和技术），然而该方法不包括对新基础设施的投资，也不能保证更好的区域凝聚力，虽然可以实现更大程度的解耦，但道路运输仍将占据市场的主导地位并继续集中在饱和的动脉线路上，尽管它是最污染的交通方式，因此这并不足以保证必要的平衡转移。', 'I': '这段话主要讲述了通过一系列措施，从定价到振兴替代交通方式，以及在欧洲跨国网络上进行投资，实现市场份额的转移，打破道路交通增长与经济增长之间的联系，而不限制人员和货物的流动。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: It is difficult to conceive of vigorous economic growth without an efficient transport system. Although modern information technologies can reduce the demand for physical transport by facilitating teleworking and teleservices, the requirement for transport continues to increase. There are two key factors behind this trend. For passenger transport, the determining factor is the spectacular growth in car use. The number of cars on European Union (EU) roads saw an increase of three million cars each year from 1990 to 2010, and in the next decade the EU will see a further substantial increase in its fleet.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: As far as goods transport is concerned, growth is due to a large extent to changes in the European economy and its system of production. In the last 20 years, as internal frontiers have been abolished, the EU has moved from a 'stock' economy to a 'flow' economy. This phenomenon has been emphasised by the relocation of some industries, particularly those which are labour intensive, to reduce production costs, even though the production site is hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away from the final assembly plant or away from users.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The strong economic growth expected in countries which are candidates for entry to the EU will also increase transport flows, in particular road haulage traffic. In 1998, some of these countries already exported more than twice their 1990 volumes and imported more than five times their 1990 volumes. And although many candidate countries inherited a transport system which encourages rail, the distribution between modes has tipped sharply in favour of road transport since the 1990s. Between 1990 and 1998, road haulage increased by 19.4%, while during the same period rail haulage decreased by 43.5%, although – and this could benefit the enlarged EU – it is still on average at a much higher level than in existing member states.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: However, a new imperative – sustainable development – offers an opportunity for adapting the EU's common transport policy. This objective, agreed by the Gothenburg European Council, has to be achieved by integrating environmental considerations into Community policies, and shifting the balance between modes of transport lies at the heart of its strategy. The ambitious objective can only be fully achieved by 2020, but proposed measures are nonetheless a first essential step towards a sustainable transport system which will ideally be in place in 30 years' time, that is by 2040.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: In 1998, energy consumption in the transport sector was to blame for 28% of emissions of CO2, the leading greenhouse gas. According to the latest estimates, if nothing is done to reverse the traffic growth trend, CO2 emissions from transport can be expected to increase by around 50% to 1,113 billion tonnes by 2020, compared with the 739 billion tonnes recorded in 1990. Once again, road transport is the main culprit since it alone accounts for 84% of the CO2 emissions attributable to transport. Using alternative fuels and improving energy efficiency is thus both an ecological necessity and a technological challenge.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: At the same time greater efforts must be made to achieve a modal shift. Such a change cannot be achieved overnight, all the less so after over half a century of constant deterioration in favour of road. This has reached such a pitch that today rail freight services are facing marginalisation, with just 8% of market share, and with international goods trains struggling along at an average speed of 18km/h. Three possible options have emerged.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The first approach would consist of focusing on road transport solely through pricing. This option would not be accompanied by complementary measures in the other modes of transport. In the short term it might curb the growth in road transport through the better loading ratio of goods vehicles and occupancy rates of passenger vehicles expected as a result of the increase in the price of transport. However, the lack of measures available to revitalise other modes of transport would make it impossible for more sustainable modes of transport to take up the baton.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The second approach also concentrates on road transport pricing but is accompanied by measures to increase the efficiency of the other modes (better quality of services, logistics, technology). However, this approach does not include investment in new infrastructure, nor does it guarantee better regional cohesion. It could help to achieve greater uncoupling than the first approach, but road transport would keep the lion's share of the market and continue to concentrate on saturated arteries, despite being the most polluting of the modes. It is therefore not enough to guarantee the necessary shift of the balance.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The third approach, which is not new, comprises a series of measures ranging from pricing to revitalising alternative modes of transport and targeting investment in the trans-European network. This integrated approach would allow the market shares of the other modes to return to their 1998 levels and thus make a shift of balance. It is far more ambitious than it looks, bearing in mind the historical imbalance in favour of roads for the last fifty years, but would achieve a market break in the link between road transport growth and economic growth, without placing restrictions on the mobility of people and goods.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 14-21

The text has 9 sections.
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

Example Answer
Paragraph F vii: The need to achieve transport balance

list.text: A fresh and important long-term goal
Charging for roads and improving other transport methods
changes affecting the distances goods may be transported
Taking all the steps necessary to change transport patterns
The environmental costs of road transport
The escalating cost of rail transport
The need to achieve transport rebalance
The rapid growth of private transport
Plans to develop major road networks
Restricting road use through charging policies alone
Transport trends in countries awaiting EU admission

questions.analysis: Section A的内容主要讨论了私家车使用的显著增长，这与小标题viii: "The rapid growth of private transport"直接对应。通过识别文章中的关键词和增长趋势，学生可以更容易地选择正确的标题。避免错选的关键在于仔细阅读每个部分的内容，并确保选择的标题与该部分的主要内容一致。

central_sentences.sentence: For passenger transport, the determining factor is the spectacular growth in car use.
central_sentences.translation: 对于客运而言，决定性因素是私家车使用的显著增长。

central_sentences.sentence: The number of cars on European Union (EU) roads saw an increase of three million cars each year from 1990 to 2010, and in the next decade the EU will see a further substantial increase in its fleet.
central_sentences.translation: 从1990年到2010年，欧盟道路上的汽车数量每年增加300万辆，在接下来的十年里，欧盟的汽车保有量将进一步大幅增加。

target_sen.target_sen: For passenger transport, the determining factor is the spectacular growth in car use.
For passenger transport, the determining factor is the spectacular growth in car use.
For passenger transport, the determining factor is the spectacular growth in car use.
For passenger transport, the determining factor is the spectacular growth in car use.

questions.analysis: 这段文字主要讨论了货物运输的变化及其原因，特别是由于欧洲经济和生产系统的变化，导致了生产地点与最终装配厂或用户之间的距离增加。这与小标题 iii: changes affecting the distances goods may be transported 完全吻合。

central_sentences.sentence: As far as goods transport is concerned, growth is due to a large extent to changes in the European economy and its system of production. In the last 20 years, as internal frontiers have been abolished, the EU has moved from a 'stock' economy to a 'flow' economy. This phenomenon has been emphasised by the relocation of some industries, particularly those which are labour intensive, to reduce production costs, even though the production site is hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away from the final assembly plant or away from users.
central_sentences.translation: 就货物运输而言，其增长在很大程度上是由于欧洲经济及其生产系统的变化。在过去20年里，随着内部边界的取消，欧盟从"存货"经济转变为"流动"经济。这种现象因一些产业（特别是劳动密集型产业）的迁移而加剧，尽管生产地点距离最终装配厂或用户可能有数百甚至数千公里之远，但这样做是为了降低生产成本。

syn_ary.synonym: changes in the European economy and its system of production

target_sen.target_sen: As far as goods transport is concerned, growth is due to a large extent to changes in the European economy and its system of production.

syn_ary.synonym: hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away

target_sen.target_sen: This phenomenon has been emphasised by the relocation of some industries, particularly those which are labour intensive, to reduce production costs, even though the production site is hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away from the final assembly plant or away from users.
As far as goods transport is concerned, growth is due to a large extent to changes in the European economy and its system of production.

questions.analysis: Section C主要讨论了候选国家的运输趋势，包括公路货运和铁路货运的变化。这与小标题xi: Transport trends in countries awaiting EU admission完全匹配。段落中提到的“countries which are candidates for entry to the EU”和“increase transport flows”等关键词与小标题中的“countries awaiting EU admission”和“Transport trends”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: The strong economic growth expected in countries which are candidates for entry to the EU will also increase transport flows, in particular road haulage traffic.
central_sentences.translation: 预计欧盟候选国家的强劲经济增长也将增加运输流量，特别是公路货运交通。

target_sen.target_sen: The strong economic growth expected in countries which are candidates for entry to the EU will also increase transport flows, in particular road haulage traffic.
The strong economic growth expected in countries which are candidates for entry to the EU will also increase transport flows, in particular road haulage traffic.
In 1998, some of these countries already exported more than twice their 1990 volumes and imported more than five times their 1990 volumes.
In 1998, some of these countries already exported more than twice their 1990 volumes and imported more than five times their 1990 volumes.
And although many candidate countries inherited a transport system which encourages rail, the distribution between modes has tipped sharply in favour of road transport since the 1990s.

Between 1990 and 1998, road haulage increased by 19.4%, while during the same period rail haulage decreased by 43.5%, although – and this could benefit the enlarged EU – it is still on average at a much higher level than in existing member states.

Between 1990 and 1998, road haulage increased by 19.4%, while during the same period rail haulage decreased by 43.5%, although – and this could benefit the enlarged EU – it is still on average at a much higher level than in existing member states.

syn_ary.synonym: countries which are candidates for entry to the EU

target_sen.target_sen: The strong economic growth expected in countries which are candidates for entry to the EU will also increase transport flows, in particular road haulage traffic.

questions.analysis: Section D主要讨论了一个新的、重要的长期目标，即可持续发展。段落中提到的 "a new imperative" 和 "an opportunity for adapting the EU's common transport policy" 对应了标题中的 "A fresh and important long-term goal"。此外，段落中提到的 "The ambitious objective can only be fully achieved by 2020" 也强调了这是一个长期目标。因此，Section D对应的小标题是i: A fresh and important long-term goal。

central_sentences.sentence: However, a new imperative – sustainable development – offers an opportunity for adapting the EU's common transport policy.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，一个新的必要任务——可持续发展——为调整欧盟共同交通政策提供了机会。

central_sentences.sentence: The ambitious objective can only be fully achieved by 2020, but proposed measures are nonetheless a first essential step towards a sustainable transport system which will ideally be in place in 30 years' time, that is by 2040.
central_sentences.translation: 这个雄心勃勃的目标只能在2020年才能完全实现，但提出的措施无疑是朝着可持续交通系统迈出的第一个重要步骤，理想情况下，这个系统将在30年后，也就是2040年建立。

target_sen.target_sen: However, a new imperative – sustainable development – offers an opportunity for adapting the EU's common transport policy.
However, a new imperative – sustainable development – offers an opportunity for adapting the EU's common transport policy.
However, a new imperative – sustainable development – offers an opportunity for adapting the EU's common transport policy.
However, a new imperative – sustainable development – offers an opportunity for adapting the EU's common transport policy.

The ambitious objective can only be fully achieved by 2020, but proposed measures are nonetheless a first essential step towards a sustainable transport system which will ideally be in place in 30 years' time, that is by 2040.

The ambitious objective can only be fully achieved by 2020, but proposed measures are nonetheless a first essential step towards a sustainable transport system which will ideally be in place in 30 years' time, that is by 2040.

This objective, agree by the Gothenburg European Council, has to be achieved by integrating environmental considerations into Community policies, and shifting the balance between modes of transport lies at the heart of its strategy.

This objective, agree by the Gothenburg European Council, has to be achieved by integrating environmental considerations into Community policies, and shifting the balance between modes of transport lies at the heart of its strategy.

The ambitious objective can only be fully achieved by 2020, but proposed measures are nonetheless a first essential step towards a sustainable transport system which will ideally be in place in 30 years' time, that is by 2040.

questions.analysis: Section E的核心内容是讨论道路运输对环境的影响，特别是CO2排放的问题。选项v "The environmental costs of road transport" 完全对应文章内容，因此是正确答案。学生可以通过识别文中关于CO2排放和环境影响的关键词，以及选项中的同义词来确定答案。避免误选其他选项的方法是注意文章主要讨论的主题和选项内容的具体细节是否匹配。

central_sentences.sentence: Once again, road transport is the main culprit since it alone accounts for 84% of the CO2 emissions attributable to transport.
central_sentences.translation: 再次强调，道路运输是主要罪魁祸首，因为它单独就占了交通运输相关二氧化碳排放的84%。

target_sen.target_sen: In 1998, energy consumption is the transport sector was to blame for 28% of emissions of CO2, the leading greenhouse gas.
In 1998, energy consumption is the transport sector was to blame for 28% of emissions of CO2, the leading greenhouse gas.
Once again, road transport is the main culprit since it alone accounts for 84% of the CO2 emissions attributable to transport.

questions.analysis: 这段文字明确提到第一种方法是“仅通过定价来关注道路运输”，这与题干中的“Restricting road use through charging policies alone”完全一致。这里的“solely through pricing”和“charging policies alone”是同义表达，表明仅通过收费政策来限制道路使用。

central_sentences.sentence: The first approach would consist of focusing on road transport solely through pricing. This option would not be accompanied by complementary measures in the other modes of transport.
central_sentences.translation: 第一种方法将只通过定价来关注道路运输。这个选择不会伴随其他交通方式的配套措施。

target_sen.target_sen: The first approach would consist of focusing on road transport solely through pricing.
The first approach would consist of focusing on road transport solely through pricing.

questions.analysis: Section G 讨论了对道路运输进行收费，并采取措施提高其他运输方式的效率。这与小标题 ii: "Charging for roads and improving other transport methods" 完全一致。文中提到的 "road transport pricing" 和 "increase the efficiency of the other modes" 分别对应小标题中的 "charging for roads" 和 "improving other transport methods"。

central_sentences.sentence: The second approach also concentrates on road transport pricing but is accompanied by measures to increase the efficiency of the other modes (better quality of services, logistics, technology).
central_sentences.translation: 第二种方法也着重于道路运输收费，但同时伴随着提高其他运输方式效率的措施（更好的服务质量、物流和技术）。

target_sen.target_sen: The second approach also concentrates on road transport pricing but is accompanied by measures to increase the efficiency of the other modes (better quality of services, logistics, technology).

syn_ary.synonym: increase the efficiency of the other modes

target_sen.target_sen: The second approach also concentrates on road transport pricing but is accompanied by measures to increase the efficiency of the other modes (better quality of services, logistics, technology).

questions.analysis: Section H讨论了第三种方法，即采取一系列综合措施来改变运输模式。这些措施包括定价、振兴替代运输方式和投资于跨欧洲网络。文章明确提到这些措施的目标是实现运输模式的平衡转变。因此，Section H对应的小标题是iv: Taking all the steps necessary to change transport patterns。

central_sentences.sentence: The third approach, which is not new, comprises a series of measures ranging from pricing to revitalising alternative modes of transport and targeting investment in the trans-European network.
central_sentences.translation: 第三种方法并不新颖，它包括一系列措施，从定价到振兴替代运输方式，以及针对跨欧洲网络的投资。

target_sen.target_sen: The third approach, which is not new, comprises a series of measures ranging from pricing to revitalising alternative modes of transport and targeting investment in the trans-European network.

syn_ary.synonym: ranging from pricing to revitalising alternative modes of transport and targeting investment in the trans-European network

target_sen.target_sen: The third approach, which is not new, comprises a series of measures ranging from pricing to revitalising alternative modes of transport and targeting investment in the trans-European network.
This integrated approach would allow the market shares of the other mode to return to their 1998 levels and thus make a shift of balance.

groups.desc:
Questions 22-26

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 2?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The need for transport is growing, despite technological developments.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到尽管有现代信息技术（technological developments），对交通运输的需求（requirement for transport）仍在增加（continues to increase）。通过对题干和文章中的关键词进行对比，可以找到直接对应的表达来确认答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Although modern information technologies can reduce the demand for physical transport by facilitating teleworking and teleservices, the requirement for transport continues to increase.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管现代信息技术通过促进远程工作和远程服务可以减少对实体交通的需求，但对交通运输的需求仍在持续增加。

target_sen.target_sen: Although modern information technologies can reduce the demand for physical transport by facilitating teleworking and teleservices, the requirement for transport continues to increase.
Although modern information technologies can reduce the demand for physical transport by facilitating teleworking and teleservices, the requirement for transport continues to increase.
Although modern information technologies can reduce the demand for physical transport by facilitating teleworking and teleservices, the requirement for transport continues to increase.

questions.title: To reduce production costs, some industries have been moved closer to their relevant consumers.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是 "为了降低生产成本，一些行业被搬迁到更靠近相关消费者的地方"。然而，原文中明确指出，尽管一些行业为了降低生产成本而搬迁，但这些生产地点实际上是远离用户的。因此，题干中的信息与文章内容相反，正确答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: This phenomenon has been emphasised by the relocation of some industries, particularly those which are labour intensive, to reduce production costs, even though the production site is hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away from the final assembly plant or away from users.
central_sentences.translation: 这种现象突出表现在一些行业的搬迁上，尤其是劳动密集型产业，尽管生产地点可能远离最终装配厂或用户数百甚至数千公里，但这样做是为了降低生产成本。

target_sen.target_sen: This phenomenon has been emphasised by the relocation of some industries, particularly those which are labour intensive, to reduce production costs, even though the production site is hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away from the final assembly plant or away from users.

This phenomenon has been emphasised by the relocation of some industries, particularly those which are labour intensive, to reduce production costs, even though the production site is hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away from the final assembly plant or away from users.

This phenomenon has been emphasised by the relocation of some industries, particularly those which are labour intensive, to reduce production costs, even though the production site is hundreds or even thousands of kilometres away from the final assembly plant or away from users.

questions.title: Cars are prohibitively expensive in some EU candidate countries.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Cars are prohibitively expensive in some EU candidate countries”在文章中找不到明确对应的信息。虽然文章提到了欧盟候选国的经济增长、运输流量以及交通方式的转变，但没有提到这些国家汽车的价格。因此，答案是 "NOT GIVEN"。即使学生词汇量有限，通过抓住关键词并在文章中定位相关段落，他们也可以发现文中没有提到汽车价格，进而得出正确答案。

central_sentences.reason: 未提及汽车价格或可负担性
central_sentences.analysis: 全文未提候选国汽车价格，故NOT GIVEN。

questions.title: The Gothenburg European Council was set up 30 years ago.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词是 "Gothenburg European Council" 和 "30 years ago"。在原文中，我们找到了关于 "Gothenburg European Council" 的信息，但没有找到任何关于 "30 years ago" 的信息。因此，文章中没有提到哥德堡欧洲理事会成立的具体时间。根据题目要求，如果信息在文章中找不到，答案应该是 "NOT GIVEN"。这就是为什么答案是 "NOT GIVEN" 的原因。

central_sentences.sentence: This objective, agree by the Gothenburg European Council, has to be achieved by integrating environmental considerations into Community policies, and shifting the balance between modes of transport lies at the heart of its strategy.
central_sentences.translation: 这个由哥德堡欧洲理事会同意的目标必须通过将环境考量纳入共同体政策来实现，而交通方式之间的平衡转变是其策略的核心。

target_sen.target_sen: This objective, agree by the Gothenburg European Council, has to be achieved by integrating environmental considerations into Community policies, and shifting the balance between modes of transport lies at the heart of its strategy.

questions.title: By the end of this decade, CO2 emissions from transport are predicted to reach 739 billion tonnes.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "By the end of this decade, CO2 emissions from transport are predicted to reach 739 billion tonnes." 与原文中的信息 "CO2 emissions from transport can be expected to increase by around 50% to 1,113 billion tonnes by 2020, compared with the 739 billion tonnes recorded in 1990." 相矛盾。原文明确指出到2020年预计的排放量是1113亿吨，而不是739亿吨。因此，题干中的信息是错误的，正确答案为 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: According to the latest estimates, if nothing is done to reverse the traffic growth trend, CO2 emissions from transport can be expected to increase by around 50% to 1,113 billion tonnes by 2020, compared with the 739 billion tonnes recorded in 1990.
central_sentences.translation: 根据最新估计，如果不采取措施扭转交通增长趋势，到2020年，交通运输的二氧化碳排放量预计将增加约50%，达到1,113亿吨，相比1990年记录的739亿吨。

target_sen.target_sen: According to the latest estimates, if nothing is done to reverse the traffic growth trend, CO2 emissions from transport can be expected to increase by around 50% to 1,113 billion tonnes by 2020, compared with the 739 billion tonnes recorded in 1990.

locating_words.locating_word: By the end of this decade

target_sen.target_sen: According to the latest estimates, if nothing is done to reverse the traffic growth trend, CO2 emissions from transport can be expected to increase by around 50% to 1,113 billion tonnes by 2020, compared with the 739 billion tonnes recorded in 1990.

According to the latest estimates, if nothing is done to reverse the traffic growth trend, CO2 emissions from transport can be expected to increase by around 50% to 1,113 billion tonnes by 2020, compared with the 739 billion tonnes recorded in 1990.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Innovation is key to business survival, and companies put substantial resources into inspiring employees to develop new ideas. There are, nevertheless, people working in luxurious, state-of-the-art centres designed to stimulate innovation who find that their environment doesn't make them feel at all creative. And there are those who don't have a budget, or much space, but who innovate successfully.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: For Robert B. Cialdini, Professor of Psychology at Arizona State University, one reason that companies don't succeed as often as they should is that innovation starts with recruitment. Research shows that the fit between an employee's values and a company's values makes a difference to what contribution they make and whether, two years after they join, they're still at the company. Studies at Harvard Business School show that, although some individuals may be more creative than others, almost every individual can be creative in the right circumstances.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: One of the most famous photographs in the story of rock'n'roll emphasises Cialdini's view. The 1956 picture of singers Elvis Presley, Carl Perkins, Johnny Cash and Jerry Lee Lewis jamming at a piano in Sun Studios in Memphis tells a hidden story. Sun's 'million-dollar quartet' could have been a quintet. Missing from the picture is Roy Orbison, a greater natural singer than Lewis, Perkins or Cash. Sam Phillips, who owned Sun, wanted to revolutionise popular music with songs that fused black and white music, and country and blues. Presley, Cash, Perkins and Lewis instinctively understood Phillips's ambition and believed in it. Orbison wasn't inspired by the goal, and only ever achieved one hit with the Sun label.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The value fit matters, says Cialdini, because innovation is, in part, a process of change, and under that pressure we, as a species, behave differently. 'When things change, we are hard-wired to play it safe.' Managers should therefore adopt an approach that appears counter-intuitive – they should explain what stands to be lost if the company fails to seize a particular opportunity. Studies show that we invariably take more gambles when threatened with a loss than when offered a reward.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Managing innovation is a delicate art. It's easy for a company to be pulled in conflicting directions as the marketing, product development, and finance departments each get different feedback from different sets of people. And without a system which ensures collaborative exchanges within the company, it's also easy for small 'pockets of innovation' to disappear. Innovation is a contact sport. You can't brief people just by saying, 'We're going in this direction and I'm going to take you with me.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Cialdini believes that this 'follow-the-leader syndrome' is dangerous, not least because it encourages bosses to go it alone. 'It's been scientifically proven that three people will be better than one at solving problems, even if that one person is the smartest person in the field.' To prove his point, Cialdini cites an interview with molecular biologist James Watson. Watson, together with Francis Crick, discovered the structure of DNA, the genetic information carrier of all living organisms. 'When asked how they had cracked the code ahead of an array of highly accomplished rival investigators, he said something that stunned me. He said he and Crick had succeeded because they were aware that they weren't the most intelligent of the scientists pursuing the answer. The smartest scientist was called Rosalind Franklin who, Watson said, "was so intelligent she rarely sought advice".'

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Teamwork taps into one of the basic drivers of human behavior. 'The principle of social proof is so pervasive that we don't even recognise it,' says Cialdini. 'If your project is being resisted, for example, by a group of veteran employees, ask another old-timer to speak up for it.' Cialdini is not alone in advocating this strategy. Research shows that peer power, used horizontally not vertically, is much more powerful than any boss's speech.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Writing, visualizing and prototyping can stimulate the flow of new ideas. Cialdini cites scores of research papers and historical events that prove that even something as simple as writing deepens every individual's engagement in the project. It is, he says, the reason why all those competitions on breakfast cereal packets encouraged us to write in saying, in no more than 10 words: 'I like Kellogg's Corn Flakes because...' The very act of writing makes us more likely to believe it.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Authority doesn't have to inhibit innovation but it often does. The wrong kind of leadership will lead to what Cialdini calls 'captainitis,' the regrettable tendency of team members to opt out of team responsibilities that are properly theirs. He calls it captainitis because, he says, 'crew members of multipilot aircraft exhibit a sometimes deadly passivity when the flight captain makes a clearly wrong-headed decision.' This behavior is not, he says, unique to air travel, but can happen in any workplace where the leader is overbearing.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: At the other end of the scale is the 1980s Memphis design collective, a group of young designers for whom 'the only rule was that there were no rules'. This environment encouraged a free interchange of ideas, which led to more creativity with form, function, colour and materials that revolutionised attitudes to furniture design.

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Many theorists believe the ideal boss should lead from behind, taking pride in collective accomplishment and giving credit where it is due. Cialdini says: 'Leaders should encourage everyone to contribute and simultaneously assure all concerned that every recommendation is important to making the right decision and will be given full attention.' The frustrating thing about innovation is that there are many approaches, but no magic formula. However, a manager who wants to create a truly innovative culture can make their job a lot easier by recognising these psychological realities.

questions.title: The example of the 'million dollar quartet' underlines the writer's point about
questions.analysis: 在文章中，作者通过“million dollar quartet”的例子来说明拥有共同目标的重要性。普雷斯利、卡什、帕金斯和刘易斯都理解并认同菲利普斯的目标，而奥比森则没有被这个目标激励。这表明了拥有共同目标（shared objective）对于创新和成功的重要性。因此，选项C是正确的。

central_sentences.sentence: Presley, Cash, Perkins and Lewis instinctively understood Phillips's ambition and believed in it. Orbison wasn't inspired by the goal, and only ever achieved one hit with the Sun label.
central_sentences.translation: Presley、Cash、Perkins和Lewis本能地理解并相信Phillips的抱负。Orbison并没有被这个目标所激励，在Sun唱片公司只创作了一首热门歌曲。

target_sen.target_sen: Sun's 'million-dollar quartet' could have been a quintet.

questions.title: James Watson suggests that he and Francis Crick won the race to discover the DNA code because they

options.text: were conscious of their own limitations.
brought complementary skills to their partnership.
were determined to outperform their brighter rivals.
encouraged each other to realise their joint ambition.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是A，因为文章明确指出，James Watson认为他和Francis Crick之所以能够成功，是因为他们意识到自己并不是最聪明的科学家。这与选项A中的 "were conscious of their own limitations" 完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: He said he and Crick had succeeded because they were aware that they weren't the most intelligent of the scientists pursuing the answer.
central_sentences.translation: 他说他和克里克之所以成功，是因为他们意识到自己并不是追求答案的科学家中最聪明的。

target_sen.target_sen: To prove his point, Cialdini cites an interview with molecular biologist James Watson.
Watson, together with Francis Crick, discovered the structure of DNA, the genetic information carrier of all living organisms.
'When asked how they had cracked the code ahead of an array of highly accomplished rival investigators, he said something that stunned me.

questions.title: The writer mentions competitions on breakfast cereal packets as an example of how to
questions.analysis: 题目问的是早餐麦片包装上的比赛是如何做到某件事的。文章中提到“competitions on breakfast cereal packets”时，解释了写作的行为让人们更可能相信自己写的内容，这与选项D“strengthen commitment to an idea”意思相同。因此，正确答案是D。

central_sentences.sentence: The very act of writing makes us more likely to believe it.
central_sentences.translation: 写作这个行为本身使我们更有可能相信它。

target_sen.target_sen: It is, he says, the reason why all those competitions on breakfast cereal packets encouraged us to write in saying, in no more than 10 words: 'I like Kellogg's Corn Flakes because- 'The very act of writing makes us more likely to believe it.

questions.title: In the last paragraph, the writer suggests that it is important for employees to

options.text: be aware of their company's goals.
feel that their contributions are valued.
have respect for their co-workers' achievements.
understand why certain management decisions are made.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到，领导者应该鼓励每个人做出贡献，并确保每一条建议都被重视和关注。这表明员工的贡献被重视是非常重要的。因此，正确答案是B：感到他们的贡献被重视。

central_sentences.sentence: Leaders should encourage everyone to contribute and simultaneously assure all concerned that every recommendation is important to making the right decision and will be given full attention.
central_sentences.translation: 领导者应该鼓励每个人做出贡献，并同时确保所有人知道每一条建议在做出正确决策时都是重要的，并会得到充分关注。

syn_ary.synonym: Leaders should encourage everyone to contribute

target_sen.target_sen: Cialdini says: 'Leaders should encourage everyone to contribute and simultaneously assure all concerned that every recommendation is important to making the right decision and will be given full attention.'

syn_ary.synonym: assure all concerned that every recommendation is important

target_sen.target_sen: Cialdini says: 'Leaders should encourage everyone to contribute and simultaneously assure all concerned that every recommendation is important to making the right decision and will be given full attention.'

groups.desc:
Questions 31-35
Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A-G, below.
Choose the correct answer and move it into the gap.

questions.title: Employees whose values match those of their employers are more likely to
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Employees whose values match those of their employers" 对应文章中的 "the fit between an employee's values and a company's values"。题干中的 "more likely to" 对应文章中的 "makes a difference to" 和 "whether, two years after they join, they're still at the company"。从文章中可以看到，员工如果和公司价值观一致，两年后他们仍然留在公司的可能性更大。因此，正确答案是 G. remain in their jobs。

central_sentences.sentence: Research shows that the fit between an employee's values and a company's values makes a difference to what contribution they make and whether, two years after they join, they're still at the company.
central_sentences.translation: 研究表明，员工的价值观与公司价值观的契合度影响着他们的贡献程度，以及他们加入公司两年后是否仍然留在公司。

syn_ary.synonym: fit between an employee's values and a company's values

target_sen.target_sen: Research shows that the fit between an employee's values and a company's values makes a difference to what contribution they make and whether, two years after they join, they're still at the company.
Research shows that the fit between an employee's values and a company's values makes a difference to what contribution they make and whether, two years after they join, they're still at the company.

questions.title: At times of change, people tend to
questions.analysis: 在题干中，"times of change" 和 "tend to" 是关键的线索词。在原文中，"When things change" 和 "we are hard-wired to" 是对应的同义替换词。原文明确指出在变化时期，人们倾向于选择安全的做法，即避免风险。因此，正确答案是 E: avoid risk。

central_sentences.sentence: When things change, we are hard-wired to play it safe.
central_sentences.translation: 当事物发生变化时，我们天生就倾向于选择安全的做法。

target_sen.target_sen: The value fit matters, says Cialdini, because innovation is, in part, a process of change, and under that pressure we, as a species, behave differently, 'When things change, we are hard-wired to play it safe.'

questions.title: If people are aware of what they might lose, they will often
questions.analysis: 题干中的“lose”在原文中对应的是“threatened with a loss”。原文提到“we invariably take more gambles when threatened with a loss”，意思是当人们面临损失威胁时，他们会冒更多的风险，这与选项A中的“take chances”意思相同。因此，正确答案是A。

central_sentences.sentence: Studies show that we invariably take more gambles when threatened with a loss than when offered a reward.
central_sentences.translation: 研究表明，当面临损失威胁时，我们通常会比获得奖励时更愿意冒险。

target_sen.target_sen: Studies show that we invariably take more gambles when threatened with a loss than when offered a reward.

questions.title: People working under a dominant boss are liable to
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 F: ignore their duties。题干中的 "dominant boss" 对应原文中的 "the wrong kind of leadership" 和 "overbearing leader"，而 "liable to" 对应 "tendency"。原文明确指出，错误的领导方式会导致团队成员放弃他们的职责，这与选项 F 完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: The wrong kind of leadership will lead to what Cialdini calls 'captainitis, the regrettable tendency of team members to opt out of team responsibilities that are properly theirs'.
central_sentences.translation: 错误的领导方式会导致Cialdini所称的"机长症"，即团队成员令人遗憾地倾向于放弃本应属于他们的团队责任。

target_sen.target_sen: This behavior is not, he says, unique to air travel, but can happen in any workplace where the leader is overbearing.
The wrong kind of leadership will lead to what Cialdini calls 'captainitis, the regrettable tendency of team members to opt out of team responsibilities that are properly theirs'.
The wrong kind of leadership will lead to what Cialdini calls 'captainitis, the regrettable tendency of team members to opt out of team responsibilities that are properly theirs'.

questions.title: Employees working in organisations with few rules are more likely to
questions.analysis: 题干中的“organisations with few rules”在原文中对应“the only rule was that there were no rules”，而“more likely to”对应“encouraged”。原文提到这种环境“encouraged a free interchange of ideas”，即鼓励了自由的想法交流，因此正确答案是B: share their ideas。

central_sentences.sentence: This environment encouraged a free interchange of ideas, which led to more creativity with form, function, colour and materials that revolutionised attitudes to furniture design.
central_sentences.translation: 这种环境鼓励了想法的自由交流，从而在形式、功能、颜色和材料方面产生了更多的创造力，彻底改变了人们对家具设计的态度。

syn_ary.synonym: the only rule was that there were no rules

target_sen.target_sen: At the other end of the scale is the 1980s Memphis design collective, a group of young designers for whom 'the only rule was that there were no rules'.
This environment encouraged a free interchange of ideas, which led to more creativity with form, function, colour and materials that revolutionised attitudes to furniture design.

groups.desc:
Questions 36-40
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer,
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer,
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

questions.title: The physical surroundings in which a person works play a key role in determining their creativity.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "physical surroundings" 对应文章中的 "luxurious, state-of-the-art centres" 和 "don't have a budget, or much space"。题干中的 "key role in determining their creativity" 对应文章中的 "doesn't make them feel at all creative" 和 "innovate successfully"。文章明确指出，尽管有些人在豪华环境中工作，但他们并不觉得这种环境能激发他们的创造力；而有些人在没有预算或空间的情况下却能成功创新。这表明物理环境并不是决定创造力的关键因素，因此答案是 NO / FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: There are, nevertheless, people working in luxurious, state-of-the-art centres designed to stimulate innovation who find that their environment doesn't make them feel at all creative. And there are those who don't have a budget, or much space, but who innovate successfully.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，有些人在豪华的、最先进的中心工作，这些中心旨在激发创新，但他们发现这种环境并不能让他们感到有创造力。而有些人虽然没有预算或足够的空间，却能成功地进行创新。

target_sen.target_sen: There are, nevertheless, people working in luxurious, state-of-the-art centres designed to stimulate innovation who find that their environment doesn't make them feel at all creative.

syn_ary.synonym: don't have a budget, or much space

target_sen.target_sen: And there are those who don't have a budget, or much space, but who innovate successfully.

syn_ary.synonym: doesn't make them feel at all creative

target_sen.target_sen: There are, nevertheless, people working in luxurious, state-of-the-art centres designed to stimulate innovation who find that their environment doesn't make them feel at all creative.
And there are those who don't have a budget, or much space, but who innovate successfully.

questions.title: Most people have the potential to be creative.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Most people have the potential to be creative.”意思是大多数人有创造力的潜力。原文中的“almost every individual can be creative in the right circumstances”与题干意思相同，表示几乎每个人在合适的环境下都可以有创造力。因此，题干的陈述在文章中得到了支持，答案是“YES”。

central_sentences.sentence: Studies at Harvard Business School show that, although some individuals may be more creative than others, almost every individual can be creative in the right circumstances.
central_sentences.translation: 哈佛商学院的研究表明，尽管有些人可能比其他人更具创造力，但几乎每个人在适当的环境下都能发挥创造力。

target_sen.target_sen: Studies at Harvard Business School show that, although some individuals may be more creative than others, almost every individual can be creative in the right circumstances.
Studies at Harvard Business School show that, although some individuals may be more creative than others, almost every individual can be creative in the right circumstances.
Studies at Harvard Business School show that, although some individuals may be more creative than others, almost every individual can be creative in the right circumstances.

questions.title: Teams work best when their members are of equally matched intelligence.
questions.analysis: 题干中的陈述是“当团队成员的智力相当时，团队工作效果最好。”然而，原文中提到的是“即使团队中有一个人是最聪明的，三个人一起解决问题也会比一个人更好。”这并没有提到团队成员需要智力相当。因此，题干中的信息在文章中没有明确提到，也没有相反的信息，所以答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: It's been scientifically proven that three people will be better than one at solving problems, even if that one person is the smartest person in the field.
central_sentences.translation: 科学已经证明，三个人解决问题会比一个人更好，即使那一个人是该领域最聪明的人。

syn_ary.synonym: three people will be better than one at solving problems

target_sen.target_sen: 'It's been scientifically proven that three people will be better than one at solving problems, even if that one person is the smartest person in the field.'

questions.title: It is easier for smaller companies to be innovative.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“smaller companies” 和“easier”在文章中没有找到明确的对应表达。虽然文章讨论了创新的重要性和影响因素，但并没有提到小公司是否更容易创新。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.reason: 缺少“smaller companies / easier”比较结论，故NOT GIVEN。
central_sentences.analysis: 提到资源少也能创新，但未比较“小公司更容易”。
central_sentences.keywords: don't have a budget, much space, innovate successfully
sentences: And there are those who don't have a budget, or much space, but who innovate successfully.
central_sentences.paraphrasing: smaller companies -> (未出现); easier -> innovate successfully with limited resources(仅现象); innovative -> innovate successfully

central_sentences.sentence: And there are those who don't have a budget, or much space, but who innovate successfully.

questions.title: A manager's approval of an idea is more persuasive than that of a colleague.
questions.analysis: 题干陈述的是“经理对一个想法的认可比同事的认可更有说服力”，而原文明确表示“同辈力量比任何老板的讲话都更有力量”。这与题干的陈述相反，因此正确答案是 NO。通过找到题干中的线索词 "manager's approval"、"colleague" 和 "more persuasive"，并在原文中找到对应的同义替换词 "boss's speech"、"peer" 和 "much more powerful"，可以确定答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Research shows that peer power, used horizontally not vertically, is much more powerful than any boss`s speech
central_sentences.translation: 研究表明，同辈力量（横向使用而不是纵向使用）比任何老板的讲话都更有力量。

target_sen.target_sen: Research shows that peer power, used horizontally not vertically, is much more powerful than any boss`s speech.
Research shows that peer power, used horizontally not vertically, is much more powerful than any boss`s speech.
Research shows that peer power, used horizontally not vertically, is much more powerful than any boss`s speech.
